description: A plain black-and-white candid documentary photograph of an industrial workplace, with no graphic design, text, seal, or border applied. The image shows a single male worker standing in the center of a large factory/fabrication shop, wearing a backwards ball cap, safety glasses, a light short-sleeve T-shirt, dark pants and work gloves, and holding what appears to be a spray gun or wand connected to a hose. To his left are large cylindrical metal tank/pipe assemblies wrapped partly in protective film; overhead are exposed ducts, beams, lighting and shop equipment. The monochrome treatment gives it a gritty, dignified 'labor documentary' feel, but it is an undesigned candid photo rather than a meme/poster. Per the tweet ('American Energy Dominance won't be achieved without our Skilled Workers. These guys are Heroes'), DOL uses it to valorize skilled energy-sector workers. Target audience: workers and the general public.
